stemHtml: The Automatic Direction Finder (ADF) is a navigation instrument which determines the direction (relative bearing) of a radio signal, usually from a ground based station. Which of the following statements accurately describes the ADF?

options.a: ADF receivers operate in the 1 MHz to 10 MHz range and can receive bearing information.
options.b: ADF receivers operate in the 190 kHz to 1750 kHz range and can receive bearing information from compatible NDB (Non-Directional Beacon) stations.
options.c: ADF receivers operate in the 110 MHz to 136 MHz range, can receive bearing information, but require a visual reference to the station.
options.d: ADF receivers operate in the 190 kHz to 535 kHz range and cannot receive bearing information.
